agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-window commitment, premium pricing, and a refund policy for missed windows. Spans order placement, dispatch, driver assignment, real-time tracking, and post-delivery financial reconciliation.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a sub-domain of Order Fulfillment that introduces time-sensitive delivery commitments, eligibility constraints, premium pricing, and SLA-driven refund workflows. Touches Order, Dispatch, Driver, Tracking, and Billing contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option with a target delivery window of 2 hours from order placement, available to eligible customers for an additional fee."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"A $12.99 surcharge applied to orders that select the Express Delivery option."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery fee charged on all orders, independent of the Express Fee. Not subject to express-related refunds."},{"term":"Delivery Window","definition":"The 2-hour target timeframe within which an express delivery should be completed, starting from order placement."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute window from order placement within which a driver must physically pick up the express order from the warehouse."},{"term":"Warehouse Proximity","definition":"The 10km radius from a warehouse within which a customer is eligible to select Express Delivery."},{"term":"Driver Rating Threshold","definition":"The minimum average rating (4.0 stars) a driver must hold to be eligible for express order assignment."},{"term":"Missed Window","definition":"The event where a delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, potentially triggering a goodwill refund."},{"term":"Goodwill Refund","definition":"A discretionary refund of the Express Fee (not the Base Delivery Fee) issued when the delivery window is missed, subject to exclusion conditions (e.g., wrong customer address)."},{"term":"Driver Cancellation","definition":"The event where an assigned express driver cancels mid-delivery, triggering a replacement assignment workflow."},{"term":"Replacement Window","definition":"The 10-minute window within which a replacement driver must be assigned after a driver cancellation before an automatic refund is triggered."},{"term":"Real-Time Tracking","definition":"Live location tracking of the assigned express driver, visible to the customer throughout the delivery lifecycle."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express Delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — the 2-hour window is impossible to meet beyond this radius given operational logistics. Violating this corrupts the eligibility model.","testable_assertion":"Given a customer address >10km from all warehouses, the Express Delivery option must not be selectable."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The Express Fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the Base Delivery Fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing is a business policy that can be changed by product/finance leadership.","testable_assertion":"When a customer selects Express Delivery, exactly $12.99 is added to the order total as a separate line item."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"An express order must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Operational constraint from the Operations Lead — exceeding 15 minutes makes the 2-hour window unachievable. This is a physical/logistical hard constraint, not a policy preference.","testable_assertion":"If pickup has not occurred within 15 minutes of order placement, the system must escalate (exact escalation behavior TBD — see escalations)."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Only drivers with an average rating of 4.0 stars or higher may be assigned to express orders.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Rating threshold is an operational quality policy that management could adjust.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with a rating below 4.0, the system must exclude them from express order assignment."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"When the delivery window is missed, only the Express Fee is refunded — never the Base Delivery Fee.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a financial boundary that protects cost recovery. Refunding the base fee would change the entire economic model and must be a deliberate executive decision, not a system default.","testable_assertion":"Given a missed delivery window triggering a refund, the refund amount equals exactly the Express Fee and no more."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"No refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, even if the delivery window is missed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is an exclusion policy that could be adjusted for customer goodwill at management discretion.","testable_assertion":"Given a missed window where the delivery address was flagged as incorrect by the driver, no refund is issued."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or an automatic refund of the Express Fee is triggered.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-minute threshold and automatic refund are operational policies, not physical constraints.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver cancellation at time T, if no replacement driver is assigned by T+10 minutes, the Express Fee is automatically refunded."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Customers must be able to track their express driver's location in real time from assignment through delivery.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"UX feature requirement, not a data integrity invariant. Could be degraded gracefully.","testable_assertion":"Given an active express delivery, the customer can view the driver's live location on the tracking screen with updates at least every 30 seconds."}],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer is exactly 10km from warehouse","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — is the 10km boundary inclusive or exclusive? Must be defined. Recommendation: inclusive (<=10km)."},{"scenario":"No drivers with 4+ stars available when express order is placed","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — should the order be queued, should express be shown as unavailable, or should the rating threshold be relaxed? Needs product decision."},{"scenario":"Pickup happens at 14 minutes but delivery still misses the 2-hour window","expected_behavior":"Standard missed-window refund policy applies — the pickup SLA is a dispatch optimization constraint, not an exclusion from refund eligibility."},{"scenario":"Driver cancels at 1 hour 55 minutes into the delivery window","expected_behavior":"Replacement window of 10 minutes would extend past the 2-hour delivery window. Both the missed window refund AND the replacement failure refund would apply — but the refund amount is the same (Express Fee only). No double-refund."},{"scenario":"Customer changes address mid-delivery to a location >10km from warehouse","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — does express commitment survive an address change? Recommendation: express commitment voids on customer-initiated address change."},{"scenario":"Multiple driver cancellations on the same order","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — does each cancellation restart the 10-minute replacement window? At what point is the order auto-cancelled entirely?"},{"scenario":"Warehouse runs out of stock after express order is placed","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — not addressed by any stakeholder. Full refund (including base fee) likely warranted since no delivery occurs at all."},{"scenario":"Wrong address determination is disputed by customer","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — who adjudicates whether the address was 'wrong'? What evidence standard? This is a customer success and legal gap."},{"scenario":"Delivery completed at exactly 2 hours 0 minutes 0 seconds","expected_behavior":"UNRESOLVED — is the boundary inclusive or exclusive? Recommendation: inclusive (delivery at exactly 2:00:00 is on-time)."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Express Fee ($12.99) creates a new revenue stream but carries refund liability. Missed-window refunds must be tracked as a cost center. If miss rates exceed ~15-20%, the product becomes margin-negative after accounting for operational overhead (premium drivers, faster dispatch). The 3-business-day refund processing creates a float benefit but may generate chargebacks if customers perceive delays.","operational":"15-minute pickup SLA requires dedicated express dispatch queue and possibly pre-positioned drivers near warehouses. 4-star driver filter reduces the eligible driver pool, potentially creating supply constraints in low-density markets. 10-minute replacement assignment requires an always-available backup driver pool or dynamic reallocation. Real-time tracking requires persistent location streaming infrastructure.","user":"Prominent 2-hour messaging sets a strong expectation. The legal vs. marketing tension (guarantee vs. best effort) creates a trust risk if customers feel misled. Real-time tracking is table-stakes for premium delivery and will be expected. Refund exclusion for wrong addresses may generate friction if not communicated clearly at checkout.","risk":"HIGH — The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is the top risk. Marketing a 'guarantee' while legally disclaiming it creates regulatory exposure (consumer protection, advertising standards) and customer trust damage. Additionally, the wrong-address exclusion without a clear adjudication process will generate support disputes.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"New premium option with strong time commitment. Trust depends on clear communication about what 'guarantee' means and transparent refund process."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"4-star rating requirement creates a tiered driver class. Express-eligible drivers may receive more orders but face higher performance pressure. Drivers below 4 stars are excluded from a revenue stream."},{"group":"Warehouse Operations","impact":"15-minute pickup SLA requires express orders to be prioritized in picking/packing. May disrupt standard order flow."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"New refund workflow, goodwill refund tracking, 3-business-day SLA for processing. Need new GL codes for express revenue and express refunds."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle disputes around wrong-address exclusions, missed-window claims, and the guarantee vs. best-effort gap. Needs clear escalation playbook."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Must resolve guarantee language before launch. Advertising 'guaranteed' delivery while disclaiming it legally is a material risk."}]},"confidence":{"level":35,"reasoning":"The spec contains a fundamental unresolved contradiction between Product/Customer Success (guarantee + automatic refund) and Legal (best effort + goodwill gesture). This is not a minor ambiguity — it determines the entire product's contract with the customer, the refund trigger logic, and the marketing language. Additionally, the automatic vs. manual refund processing conflict is unresolved. Until Legal and Product align on the commitment model, acceptance criteria cannot be reliably defined.","high_confidence_areas":["Express Fee amount ($12.99)","10km warehouse proximity eligibility","4-star driver rating threshold","Base Delivery Fee is never refunded for express misses","Wrong-address exclusion from refund"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether delivery window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA","Whether refunds are automatic or manually processed within 3 business days","Escalation behavior when pickup SLA is breached","Replacement driver pool availability and fallback behavior","Wrong-address adjudication process"],"assumptions":["The 2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup","Distance calculation is straight-line (geodesic), not driving distance — needs confirmation","Driver rating is a rolling average, not per-delivery — needs confirmation","Express is available 24/7 — no time-of-day restrictions mentioned","Only one express tier exists (no ultra-express or tiered windows)"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Product Manager and Customer Success describe the 2-hour window as a 'guarantee' with automatic refunds. Legal explicitly states it is a 'best effort SLA' and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These are mutually exclusive product models. A guarantee implies contractual obligation and deterministic refund triggers. Best-effort implies discretionary refunds. The system cannot encode both — this must be resolved before implementation.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Legal","Customer Success","Marketing","Customers"],"options":["A: Guarantee model — 2-hour commitment is contractual, refunds are automatic and deterministic, marketing says 'guaranteed'","B: Best-effort model — 2-hour target is aspirational, refunds are discretionary goodwill gestures reviewed by support, marketing says 'target' or 'aim'","C: Hybrid — system auto-refunds but ToS language frames it as goodwill, not a legal obligation (common industry pattern)"],"recommendation":"Option C (Hybrid) — Auto-refund as a system behavior to match customer expectations, but frame the legal language as a goodwill policy. This is standard practice in the courier industry (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats). However, Legal MUST sign off on the specific ToS language. Product and Legal must jointly decide before any implementation begins."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success says refund should be 'automatic' upon replacement failure. Finance says refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' These describe fundamentally different refund architectures — one is event-driven and instant, the other is batch-processed with a manual review window.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance","Customer Success","Engineering","Customers"],"options":["A: Fully automatic — system issues refund immediately upon missed window or replacement timeout","B: Auto-initiated, manually approved — system creates refund request, finance reviews and approves within 3 business days","C: Auto-refund for clear-cut cases (missed window, replacement timeout), manual review for edge cases (wrong address disputes)"],"recommendation":"Option C — Tiered approach. Auto-refund for deterministic triggers (clock-based), manual review for subjective triggers (wrong address). Finance gets their review window for edge cases, customers get instant resolution for clear misses."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No stakeholder defined what happens when the 15-minute pickup SLA is breached. Does the order get cancelled? Reassigned? Does the customer get notified? Does the clock reset? This is a critical operational gap — the pickup SLA exists to protect the 2-hour window but has no defined failure mode.","affected_stakeholders":["Operations","Customers","Drivers"],"options":["A: Auto-reassign to another driver, restart 15-minute pickup clock (risks cascading delays)","B: Notify customer of delay, offer option to cancel for full refund or continue with adjusted expectations","C: Escalate to dispatch supervisor for manual intervention"],"recommendation":"Option B — Transparency with customer choice. The 15-minute breach already jeopardizes the 2-hour window, so the customer should be informed and given agency."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No stakeholder addressed: (1) Hours of operation for express delivery, (2) Geographic rollout scope, (3) Maximum order size/weight for express, (4) Whether express is available during peak demand/bad weather, (5) How 'wrong address' is determined and by whom, (6) Whether distance is measured as straight-line or driving distance.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Operations","Customer Support","Engineering"],"options":["Conduct follow-up discovery session covering operational constraints and edge cases"],"recommendation":"Schedule a focused 30-minute session with Operations and Product to close these gaps before sprint planning. These are not nice-to-haves — items like distance calculation method and wrong-address adjudication directly affect system design."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot define acceptance criteria due to unresolved guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction","business_rule_broken":"The core product promise (BR-5, BR-7) cannot be encoded as deterministic system behavior until Legal and Product agree on the commitment model"},{"violation":"Refund trigger architecture is contradictory","business_rule_broken":"BR-7 (automatic refund on replacement failure) conflicts with Finance's 3-business-day processing requirement — system cannot implement both simultaneously without a tiered resolution model"}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-window commitment, premium pricing, and a refund policy for missed windows. Spans order placement, dispatch, driver assignment, real-time tracking, and post-delivery financial reconciliation.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a sub-domain of Order Fulfillment that introduces time-sensitive delivery commitments, eligibility constraints, premium pricing, and SLA-driven refund workflows.
